constraints: Ordinary branches run compilation fast deterministic edit-mode tests content validation generated-registry checks and affected-system tests
Integration requires relevant play-mode smoke tests and a Windows player smoke build
Milestones run full regression performance memory save recovery migration clean-machine offline-launch and settings checks
Flaky tests are quarantined and repaired
Android networking and full co-op pipelines remain deferred
Each artifact records build content source Unity dependency generated-content test symbol checksum and save-schema identity
The same artifact is promoted through test and release channels
Any change creates a new artifact
Keep current and previous known-good builds plus migration and rollback support
Formal external and Stage 2 builds are immutable and identifiable
Bounded local logs capture build session mission performance error and save-recovery outcomes
Logs remain local unless the tester explicitly exports them
Personal and unrelated system information is excluded or redacted
Logs rotate and support one-click diagnostic bundles
Remote telemetry is deferred and opt-in only if scale justifies it
Saves record game content schema migration and stable-ID information
Newer builds back up validate migrate and atomically commit supported older saves
Failed migrations restore safely and older builds refuse newer schemas
Rollback uses clones or compatible backups
Removed content uses tombstones replacements refunds or compensation
Development resets archive old profiles while public compatibility promises remain explicit
Developer builds expose reproducible setup inspection performance and fault-injection commands
Formal test builds expose only needed commands and log use
Altered sessions are excluded from ordinary evidence achievements and records
Public builds exclude progression-altering commands and retain safe support diagnostics
Save-changing diagnostics use cloned or temporary profiles
Practice accessibility and speedrun tools are legitimate product features
Pin Unity LTS packages and approved dependencies with license source version and purpose
Reject incompatible environments and automatic upgrades
Upgrades use isolated tasks with comprehensive validation and rollback
Prefer small maintained dependencies behind owned adapters
Defer analytics storefront networking and mobile SDKs until needed
Retain metadata required to reproduce promoted artifacts
Primary target sustains stable 60 FPS at 1080p in representative heavy combat
Minimum floor preserves complete readable gameplay with reduced visuals
High-end options add no gameplay advantage
Expose transparent scalable visual and display controls
Validate a small representative hardware and driver matrix
Track explicit CPU GPU allocation memory loading atlas particle enemy and projectile budgets
Test builds use immutable checksummed portable artifacts and support side-by-side versions
Public release uses a storefront or conventional installer with optional updates and complete offline launch
Campaign play requires no account
Storefront features remain adapters and a manual or DRM-free path may be offered
Updates uninstall and rollback preserve saves
Public artifacts should be signed when practical
Normal play requires no administrator rights and writes only to documented locations
Do not ship secrets and defensively validate files saves journals archives and support bundles
Sanitize exported diagnostics
Track dependencies licenses vulnerabilities and promoted-build manifests
Avoid mandatory DRM kernel anti-cheat and online activation
Modified profiles may lose protected competitive eligibility without losing campaign access
Each task uses one small declared-scope owned-file branch and one revertable change
Merges require current integration state layered CI tests documentation and generated snapshots
Shared systems saves dependencies balance and build configuration receive stronger review
Human and AI work follows the same gates and ownership rules
Generated files are rebuilt rather than manually conflict-resolved
Failures are fixed on task branches and releases come only from protected validated commits

open_questions_added: Explicit Stage 1 and Stage 2 milestone budgets and timebox review rules
Definition of done for AI-agent work items and handoffs
Asset provenance and licensing policy for generated and outsourced content
Recovery and disaster-backup policy for repository and production assets

next_action.instruction: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting how Stage 1 and Stage 2 are bounded against indefinite expansion: no hard timebox and continue until quality is satisfactory; one fixed calendar deadline for each whole stage; or milestone budgets with predeclared review caps for core mechanics environment external validation and Stage 2 end-to-end production proof, where missed caps trigger scope reduction diagnosis or stop review rather than automatic overtime. Preserve the two-stage evidence gates pivot-or-stop rule solo-led AI-assisted production one-level slice and exact thresholds as planning variables. Include calendar uncertainty part-time work hidden technical risk architecture overbuilding quality pressure sunk-cost behavior and how to handle justified extensions. Put the recommendation after all options. Begin a new D-231 through D-240 batch unless cadence changes.
